_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: A triangular sail has a base of 9 meters and a corresponding height of 6 meters. What is the area of the sail, in square meters?
author_rationale: The author computed base × height = 9 × 6 = 54 and marked 54 m². That is WRONG: it is the area of the enclosing parallelogram, not the triangle. The author forgot the factor of one-half in the triangle-area formula.

intended_defect.description: The marked key (54) omits the 1/2 factor in the triangle area formula. The stem is unambiguous — a base and its corresponding perpendicular height are given — so the error is purely one of calculation, not interpretation.
intended_defect.expected_finding: The bounded solver computes area = 1/2 × base × height = 1/2 × 9 × 6 = 27 m², contradicting the marked 54. The marked value is exactly double the true area because the 1/2 factor was dropped.

source.excerpt: The area of a triangle equals one half the product of a base and its corresponding height: A = 1/2 × b × h. The product b × h alone gives the area of the parallelogram spanned by the base and height, which is twice the triangle's area.
source.relevance: Identifies exactly the step the author dropped: multiplying by the 1/2 factor. The marked 54 = 9 × 6 is the parallelogram area; the triangle area is 1/2 of that, 27.
